behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: This is a deterministic key-derivation correctness property at a single function/object boundary. No behavior text indicates OS/network/subprocess, external side-effect readback, or AI dependence. Higher levels were considered and rejected because the behavior text is only about key generation from parameters, not cross-module handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit test for the key generator: same normalized parameter sets always yield identical keys; distinct parameter sets across a broad corpus do not collide.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: The risk is config-to-TTL wiring across modules (config source/loader + TTL computation), which unit-only tests of the calculator can miss. System was considered and rejected because behavior text mentions configured freshness rules only, with no real OS/network/subprocess or external round-trip requirement. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by no AI/model steps.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring real config parsing with TTL computation: load category freshness config, request TTL for each category, and assert computed durations match configured values and default/fallback behavior.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears in interaction between memory-pressure signal, threshold configuration, and strategy selector; this is multi-module orchestration without requiring real OS memory primitives. System was considered and rejected because behavior text only states configured threshold logic, not real OS-level memory telemetry or subprocess behavior. No AI dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with cache manager + strategy selector + config: drive memory-usage metric below and above threshold and assert active policy changes from LRU to LFU exactly at configured boundary.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: This requires proving an externally visible side effect via round-trip across a real DB boundary: write to PostgreSQL, then observe cache invalidation outcome. That matches REAL-INFRA/SYSTEM TRIAD (real external contract and round-trip side effect). Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly requires write to underlying PostgreSQL table, where mocks can hide trigger/listener/transaction timing failures.
behaviors.test_description: System test using real PostgreSQL and running cache layer: seed cache entries, perform real table write/update/delete affecting specific rows, then read through cache API and verify matching keys were invalidated while unrelated keys remain.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on interaction between cache operations (read/write/access accounting) and eviction module state, not just isolated policy functions. System was considered and rejected because behavior text does not require OS/network/subprocess or external round-trip boundary. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model decisions exist.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test on in-process cache with finite capacity: exercise access patterns to establish recency/frequency, fill cache, trigger eviction under LRU and LFU modes, and assert evicted keys match policy expectations.
